description: In an airport terminal, Rachel pushes past a man in a blue jacket. "Ross," she says.
Rachel continues pushing past travelers. "Ross, excuse me. Pardon me," she says.

Rachel approaches a Flight Representative at a podium. Rachel says, "Hi. Hi." The representative asks, "May I see your boarding pass?" Rachel shakes her head. "No, no, I don't have one. I just need to talk to my friend." The representative says, "Oh, I'm sorry."
The representative points her finger toward the jetway. "You are not allowed on the flight unless you have a boarding pass."
Rachel grips the wall next to the doorway. "No, I know that. I just... oh, he's right there. He's got the blue jacket on. Can I just" The representative interrupts, "I'm sorry, no."
The representative raises her hands. "Federal regulations."
Rachel gestures with her hands. "Okay. All right. Okay, then could you please just give him a message for me? Please?"
Rachel clasps her hands together. "This is very important."
The representative nods. "All right. What's the message?"
Rachel lowers her hands and looks to the side, her mouth slightly open.
Rachel says, "I don't," then pauses. The setting changes to a jetway. Ross walks down the corridor wearing headphones and carrying a shoulder bag. The Flight Representative walks past him.
The Flight Representative taps an older man in a blue jacket on the shoulder. "Excuse me, sir."
The older man stops and turns to face her.
The representative points at him. "I have a message for you."
The older man asks, "What?" The representative says, "It's from Rachel. She said that she loved the present and she will see you when you get back."
The older man's brow furrows as he turns toward a woman standing next to him.
The older man gestures with his hand. "Toby, for God's sake, I don't know what she's talking about."
Toby stares at him, her mouth set in a straight line.
The older man points at himself. "There's no Rachel."
The older man steps closer to Toby. "Don't give me that deep freeze."
Toby turns and walks away. The older man follows her down the jetway.
The setting changes to a nighttime exterior view of the Empire State Building. The view then shifts to an illuminated indoor model of the building.
The setting changes to a dark bedroom. Joey and Melanie lie in bed, the blankets pulled up to their chests.
Melanie leans against the headboard, breathing heavily. "Joey, Joey, Joey," she says.
Joey looks up and exhales. "I think I blacked out there for a minute."
Melanie smiles, looking at Joey.
Melanie says, "There's nothing."
Melanie shifts her body, turning more toward Joey.
Melanie smiles and rests her hand on Joey's chest. "Well, now we got to find something fun for you," she says.
Melanie leans down and kisses Joey's chest and neck. Joey closes his eyes and smiles, exhaling audibly.
Joey lifts his head slightly, opening his eyes. "You know what?"
Joey continues looking at Melanie.
Joey shakes his head slightly. "Forget about me."
Melanie looks up at him.
Joey says, "Let's give you another turn."
Melanie stares at Joey, her eyes widening.
Melanie asks, "Me, again?"
Joey smiles and nods. "Sure, why not?"
Melanie smiles broadly, resting her chin near his chest.
Melanie says, "Boy, somebody's going to get a big fruit basket tomorrow."
Melanie leans up and kisses Joey on the mouth, then kisses his neck as he wraps his arm around her.
Melanie lifts her head and looks at Joey. "I gotta tell you, Joey, you are nothing like I thought you would be."
Joey asks, "How do you mean?" Melanie replies, "I don't know."
Melanie points her finger. "I guess I just had you pegged as one of those guys who are always me, me, me."
Melanie places her hand on his chest. "But you, you're a giver."
Joey looks at her, his expression remaining attentive.
Melanie smiles. "You're like, the most generous man I ever met. I mean, you're practically a woman."
Joey's eyes widen and his smile fades slightly. He continues looking at her.
Joey's lips press into a tight smile as he nods slowly, maintaining eye contact.
Joey says, "Thanks," as he pulls Melanie into a hug, pressing his cheek against her head. His eyes remain wide.
Joey maintains the hug, his expression fixed as he stares straight ahead.
The setting changes to the exterior of a brick apartment building at night, with several illuminated windows.
Inside an apartment, Monica stands near the purple front door as it opens.
Rachel walks in, taking off her jacket. "So? I missed him."
Phoebe says, "Oh, I'm sorry." Monica adds, "That's too bad." Chandler, seated at a table, says, "You're right."
Chandler pushes his chair back and stands up.
Chandler steps away from the table. "Oh, I feel bad for her. I really do. I can think of somebody other than myself."
Chandler walks past Rachel and Monica, heading toward the door.
Chandler touches Rachel's shoulder. "All right, I'm going to bed."
Chandler reaches the door and opens it. Rachel touches her head.
Chandler looks back into the room. "Happy birthday, Rach. Hang in there." Rachel replies, "Thanks."
Chandler steps into the hallway and pulls the door shut behind him.
The door opens again and Chandler leans his head in. "Yes?"
Phoebe, Rachel, and Monica look toward the door in silence.
Chandler steps partially back into the room holding a paper. "Pizza coupons."
Rachel nods slightly, her hands clasped together in front of her.
In an apartment kitchen, Phoebe stands with her arms crossed, Rachel sits at a table with a birthday cake, and Monica stands nearby. Rachel inhales deeply and exhales audibly.
Rachel stands up and asks, "Oh, God. All right, you guys want help cleaning up this mess here?" as she picks up a stack of plates. Monica gestures with her hands and says, "That'd be great."
Phoebe adjusts her arms, then walks to the counter behind the table. Monica holds a piece of wrapping paper, looking down at it.
Monica looks at Rachel and says, "So, Rach, you want to save this wrapping paper? I mean, it's only a little bit torn. So are you going to go for it with"
Monica continues, "Ross, or should I just throw it out?" as she holds the paper up toward Rachel.
Rachel shakes her head, takes the wrapping paper from Monica, and walks toward the sink area while wringing the paper in her hands.
Rachel says, "I don't know, I don't know," as she turns back toward the table, her hands gesturing slightly.
Rachel continues walking toward the table and looks down.
Rachel gestures with her hands and says, "Thought about it all the way there and I thought about it all the way back and"
Rachel gestures with both hands in front of her.
Rachel clenches her hands near her waist and says, "Guys, you know, it's Ross. You know what I mean? I mean, it's Ross," making an outward gesture.
Rachel looks down and clasps her hands together in front of her.
Rachel shakes her head slightly, her hands still clasped, and says, "This is just my initial gut feeling,"
Rachel exhales audibly, her arms resting at her sides.
Rachel says, "but I'm thinking" while looking toward Monica and Phoebe.
Rachel looks away, then back toward Monica.
Rachel says, "oh, I'm thinking it'd be really great." Monica steps toward her and says, "Oh, my God."
Monica gasps, bringing her hands up near her face.
Monica embraces Rachel tightly and says, "We'd be like friends-in-law."
Monica pulls back from the hug, holding Rachel's hands, and begins speaking excitedly.
Monica says, "The best part is that you already know everything about him." as Rachel walks back toward the table.
Monica follows Rachel and says, "It's like starting on the 15th date." Phoebe, standing at the counter behind them, adds, "Yeah, but it would be like starting on the 15th date."
Rachel sits back down at the table and points a finger up.
Rachel says, "Another good point," while keeping her finger raised.
Phoebe steps away from the counter and turns toward Rachel, lowering her arms.
Phoebe gestures with her hands as she speaks, "When you're at the 15th date you're already in a very relationshipy place."
Phoebe nods and adds, "You're committed."
Rachel rests her chin on her hand, looking thoughtfully.
Phoebe gestures with her hands and asks, "And then what happens if it doesn't work out?"
Monica turns to Rachel, gesturing, and asks, "Why isn't it working out?" Rachel replies, "I don't know. Sometimes it doesn't."
Monica leans closer and asks rapidly, "Is he not cute enough for you? Does he not make enough money?"
Rachel waves her hand dismissively and stammers, "No, I'm just maybe there's someone else." Monica interjects, "Is"
Monica asks, "there? Is there someone else?" Rachel shakes her head and replies, "No, there is no one else."
Monica raises her hands, her voice elevating as she asks, "Then why the hell are you dumping my brother?"
Rachel recoils slightly, crossing her arms over her chest. She glances back at Phoebe. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to an exterior view of a city skyline at sunset.
The setting changes to an apartment living room. Chandler sits at a kitchen counter reading a newspaper.
Joey enters the room through a doorway wearing a robe, adjusting the belt. Chandler looks at him and says, "Hey, big spender."
Joey walks across the room, pointing back toward the door he entered from.
Joey says, "She's still asleep."
Chandler asks, "So how'd it go?" Joey gestures with his hands and replies, "It was amazing."
Joey steps closer to the counter near Chandler.
Joey says, "You know how you always think you're great in bed?" Chandler looks at him and replies, "The fact that you'd even ask that question shows how little you know me."
Joey takes a spoonful of cereal from a bowl on the counter.
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "It's like last night I couldn't do the thing that usually makes me great so I had to do all this other stuff."
Joey spreads his arms wide and says, "And the response I got, man, oh, man, was like a ticker tape parade."
Chandler looks at Joey in silence. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler points his spoon toward Joey and says, "Yes, I know. As it happens, my room is very, very close to the parade route."
Joey chuckles, pumping his fist slightly, while Chandler continues eating his cereal. Joey shakes his head and looks down as audience laughter is audible.
Joey says softly, "It was amazing."
Joey places a hand on his chest and says, "And not just for her. For me, too."
Joey gestures animatedly with his hands as he says, "It's like, all of a sudden I'm blind but all my other senses are heightened. You know? It's like I"
Joey raises his hand to eye level and continues, "was able to appreciate it on another level."
Chandler stares at Joey in silence, holding his spoon halfway to his mouth. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "I didn't know you had another level."
Joey nods slowly.
Joey replies, "I know."
Joey adds, "Neither did I."
Chandler sets his spoon down and picks up his newspaper. Joey leans closer, resting his arms on the counter.
Joey asks, "Hey, is something going on with OJ" as Chandler reads the paper.
Chandler continues to read the paper in silence while Joey looks at him.
At night, city skyscrapers with lit windows stand against a dark sky. The setting changes to a living room where Monica sits at a round table, holding a mug and a piece of mail.
Rachel walks into the room wearing a black short-sleeved top and a gray skirt. Monica turns toward her and says, "Hey, great skirt."
Monica asks, "Birthday present?" and Rachel replies, "Yeah."
Monica asks, "From who?" Rachel walks past the table toward the kitchen area and says, "From you. I exchanged the blouse you got me."
Rachel stops near the refrigerator and sets down an item. Monica turns her head to follow Rachel's movement, keeping her gaze on her.
Monica looks down at her wrist and asks, "Hey, doesn't Ross's flight get in a couple of hours at gate 27 B?" Rachel picks up a bottle from the counter.
Rachel pauses her movement and looks down, holding the bottle with both hands in front of her.
Rachel lifts her head and replies, "Yeah."
Rachel stands still, maintaining her gaze on Monica.
Rachel steps closer to the table, gesturing with one hand. She says, "Monica, you know, honey, I've been thinking about it and I've decided this whole Ross thing is just not a good idea."
Monica tilts her head slightly and asks, "Why?"
Rachel gestures with both hands in front of her chest and says, "Because I feel like I wouldn't just be going out with him. I would be going out with all of you. There would just be all..."
Rachel says, "...this—" as Monica stands up quickly, gripping Rachel's upper arms. Monica speaks rapidly, interrupting, "no pressure. No pressure?"
Monica holds Rachel's arms tightly, looking directly into her face.
Rachel says, "Monica, nothing has even happened yet, and you're already so—" Monica pulls her hands away from Rachel's arms and interrupts, "I am not so."
Monica gestures with one hand and replies, "Okay, I was a teensy bit weird at first."
Monica adds, "But I'll be good."
Monica says, "I promise." A loud buzzer sounds in the room.
Rachel turns away from Monica and walks quickly toward the apartment door.
Rachel presses a button on the wall intercom beside the door and asks, "Who is it?"
A male voice answers through the intercom, "It's me, Carl."
Rachel says into the intercom, "Come on up," and steps back from the wall.
Monica points her finger at Rachel and asks, "Behind my brother's back..."
Rachel turns around to face Monica.
Rachel points back at Monica and says, "...is exactly the kind of crazy thing you won't be hearing from me."
Monica places both hands on her hips, smiling, while Rachel looks away.
The scene changes to another apartment. Chandler sits in an armchair, completely surrounded by numerous large fruit baskets wrapped in clear cellophane and colorful ribbons.
Joey walks into the room holding a yellow slip of paper, smiling broadly.
Joey holds up the paper toward Chandler and says, "$700. All right, you did it."
Chandler looks around at the surrounding baskets and asks, "Do we have any fruit?"
Joey smiles and lowers the paper to his side.
Joey says, "Man, hell of a two weeks. You know what, though? I really feel like I learned something."
Chandler, remaining seated, responds, "Really?"
Chandler gestures outward with both hands and asks, "So you're going to stick with this it's all for her thing?"
Joey replies, "What are you crazy?"
Joey shifts his weight and steps slightly forward.
Joey spreads his arms out wide, closes his eyes, and mimics a stumbling walk. He asks, "When a blind man gets his sight back, does he walk around like this?"
Chandler nods slowly, keeping his gaze on Joey.

The scene changes to a dimly lit room with brick walls. Rachel stands behind a low brick wall holding a glass of a light-colored beverage. Opposite her stands Carl, wearing a light suit and holding a drink.
Carl gestures with his free hand and says, "I'm just saying, if I see one more picture of Ed Begley Jr..."
Carl continues, "...In that stupid electric car I'm going to shoot myself." Rachel takes a sip from her glass.
Carl raises one finger slightly, preparing to speak again.
Carl says, "I mean, don't get me wrong. I'm not against environmental issues per se. It's just that guy."
Ross is now standing beside Rachel. He looks at her and says, "I can't believe you'd rather go out with him than me."
Rachel lowers her glass and turns her head slightly toward Ross.
Rachel says, "Excuse me, please."
Rachel continues, "I'm trying to have a date here." Ross responds, "Fine. Just stop thinking about me."
Rachel raises a hand to rub her forehead, then covers her mouth, keeping her eyes cast downward.
Ross says, "Can't do it, can you?"
Rachel lowers her hand and looks directly into Ross's face.
Rachel says, "So I'm thinking about you. So what?"
Ross steps closer, leaning slightly toward her, and says, "I don't get it. What do you see in this guy anyway?"
Rachel crosses her arms over her chest.
Rachel says, "Well, he happens to be a very nice guy."
From off-screen, Carl speaks loudly, "I mean, come on, buddy. Get a real car."
Ross maintains his gaze on Rachel, who keeps her arms tightly crossed.
Ross reaches out, uncrossing Rachel's arms to take her hands in his. He says, "Rachel, come on. Give us a chance."
Rachel looks steadily at Ross.
Rachel replies, "Ross, it's too no."
Rachel says softly, "No."
Ross responds, "Why? Because it might get weird for everyone else? Who cares about them. This is about us. Look,"
Ross holds her hands, his eyes fixed on hers.
Ross continues, "I've been in love with you since, like, the 9th grade."
Rachel says, "Ross, you're, like, my best friend." Ross replies, "I know." Rachel continues, "If we broke up and I lost you..."
Ross releases one of her hands, placing his own hand against his chest, and asks, "What makes you think we're going to break up?" Rachel says, "Well, have you been..."
Rachel gestures slightly with her free hand and continues, "...involved with someone where you haven't broken up?"
Rachel looks at Ross as he keeps his hand pressed to his chest.
On a balcony at night, Rachel and Ross stand facing each other, holding hands.
Ross says, "No,"
Ross maintains his gaze on Rachel.
Ross continues, "but it only has to happen once."
Rachel and Ross stand still, maintaining eye contact.
Ross says, "Look, you and I both know we are perfect for each other."
Ross continues, "Right? So the only question is are you attracted to me?"
Rachel looks at Ross with her lips slightly parted.
Rachel says, "I don't know."
Ross keeps his eyes on Rachel, his expression neutral.
Rachel says, "I mean, I've never looked at you that way before."
Ross shifts his posture slightly.
Ross replies, "Well, start looking."
Ross leans forward, places his hands on Rachel's face, and they begin to kiss.
Ross and Rachel continue kissing, with Rachel placing her hands on his shoulders.
They sustain the kiss as Rachel moves her hands to the back of his neck. The setting then changes to another balcony where Rachel sits at a table with Carl.
Rachel says, "Wow." Carl replies, "Exactly."
Carl gestures with his hand, which holds a drink.
Carl points to himself and says, "And you just know I'm going to be the guy caught behind this hammerhead in traffic."
Carl takes a sip from his glass while Rachel looks down and then back up at him.
Rachel says, "Right."
Carl lowers his glass. Rachel places a hand on her chest.
Rachel says, "You know what?" Carl responds, "What?"
Rachel gestures with her hands and speaks quickly, "I forgot. I'm supposed to pick up a friend at the airport. I am so sorry."
Rachel continues, "If you want to stay and finish your drinks,"
Rachel finishes, "please do."
Rachel grabs her purse and stands up, turning away from the table.
Looking back at Carl, Rachel says, "I gotta go. I'm sorry."
Carl looks at her as she turns.
Carl says, "But,"
Carl watches as Rachel walks away. The setting changes to a living room where Monica sits in an armchair folding paper as Rachel hurries into the room.
Monica asks, "Hey, where are you going?" Rachel replies, "To get Ross." Monica stands up quickly, raises her hands, and says, "Oh, my God. This is so exciting."
Rachel stops near the door, adjusting a pin on her dress. She says, "Yeah, I know. Monica, listen, if you want the rest of my date, I'm not going to finish it."
Monica steps toward Rachel.
Monica says, "Rachel, he's not a sandwich."
Rachel opens the front door and replies, "No, he's an investment banker with tickets to the opera."
Monica runs to the balcony window, waves, and calls out, "Hello?" Carl is visible outside through the window.
The setting changes to the hallway outside the apartments. Chandler steps out of his door just as Rachel walks past toward the stairs.
Chandler looks at Rachel and says, "Hey, Rach, where are you?"
Chandler moves quickly toward Rachel.
Chandler grabs Rachel's shoulders and says, "Oh, no, you can't tell him. There's so much stuff I haven't done in my life."
Chandler continues, "I haven't watched Baywatch since they brought on Yasmine Bleeth."
Rachel puts her hands on Chandler's chest, looking up at him.
Rachel says, "Okay, Chandler, relax. I'm going to the airport."
Chandler releases her and steps back, saying, "Oh, well, then fly, good woman. Fly like the wind."
Rachel turns and quickly descends the stairs. Chandler points after her.
Chandler calls out, "Don't forget to tell him I'm the one who blew it."
Chandler lowers his arm.
Chandler leans his back against the wall, closes his eyes, and says, "Yes, another successful case for the love doctor."
The scene changes to a large passenger plane descending toward an illuminated runway against an orange night sky.
The plane continues its descent over the runway lights.
The plane touches down. The scene shifts to an airport terminal where Rachel stands among a crowd, holding a bouquet of flowers.
A passing man says, "Sorry." An off-screen voice says, "Hi, Toby." An older man walks by saying, "For God's sake, will you let it go?"
The older man continues walking next to a woman and says, "There's no Rachel."
Rachel remains standing, scanning the crowd. The setting shifts to a corridor where passengers are walking, and a dark-haired woman walks forward.
Ross walks beside the dark-haired woman, catches her slipping bag, and says, "Oh, hey, I got that."
The woman looks at Ross.
The woman says, "No problem."
Ross and the woman lean in and kiss each other on the lips as they walk.
An off-screen voice says, "showing."
The scene shifts back to Rachel in the terminal, smiling. The scene then cuts back to Ross and the dark-haired woman walking hand in hand.
Ross looks at the woman and says, "I cannot wait for you to meet my friends."
The woman says, "Really?" Ross replies, "Yeah." She adds, "You must think they'll judge and ridicule me."
Ross smiles and says, "No, they will. I just can't wait."
Ross gently shakes her hand and adds, "Come on, they're going to love you."
The scene returns to Rachel waiting in the terminal, her expression softening.
A song with vocals plays, singing, "Say goodbye." Rachel looks around the crowd.
Rachel continues looking around expectantly while people pass by.
Rachel's smile fades slightly as she waits in the moving crowd.

An upbeat song with vocals begins playing. At night, outdoors near a large, active fountain, Ross and Monica face each other, smiling and moving their arms. The area is illuminated by streetlights and the fountain's underwater lights.

The song vocals sing, "It's like you're always stuck in second gear." Monica and Ross continue moving. Chandler and a woman are then shown dancing rapidly, followed by Phoebe splashing water from the fountain.
The song vocals continue, "when it hasn't been your day." Ross and Chandler face each other, moving their arms rhythmically.
The song vocals sing, "your week, your month, or even your year." Joey, wearing a white turtleneck, appears briefly. Rachel and Chandler are then shown dancing rapidly.
Joey sits in a floating lounge chair inside the fountain pool, holding a tropical drink and smiling.
The song vocals sing, "But I'll be there for you." Phoebe throws her arms upward while smiling. Chandler is then shown dancing.
Rachel and a man stand together; Rachel extends her arm to the side.

The song vocals sing, "I'll be there for you 'cause you're there for me too." Ross runs forward in his suit, extending his arm, and is then shown sitting on the edge of the fountain, getting splashed by the falling water.

The instrumental music continues. Chandler and Rachel dance near the fountain. The six main characters—Ross, Monica, Chandler, Joey, Phoebe, and Rachel—are then shown standing together in front of the water, all wearing black and white clothing. They begin to sit on a couch positioned in front of the fountain.

The six characters settle onto the couch in various poses, with a lit floor lamp standing next to it. At 12:34, the setting changes to the empty fountain at night, seen from a distance. The music decreases in volume.
The screen becomes completely black, and the music stops, leaving silence.
The screen remains black and silence continues.
